evals.name: loads for explicit committee and worker loop
evals.prompt: Use two subagents: one expert committee to rate this output, and one worker to revise it until the committee gives 10/10.
evals.expected_output: The agent should set up an expert committee, a revision worker, and an iterative review loop with a target rating.
assertions: The output defines the committee role and revision worker role.
The output includes a numeric target rating such as 10/10.
The output loops review and revision until the threshold or a blocker is reached.

evals.name: loads for committee review and scoring
evals.prompt: Organize a three-expert committee to review this page, give a final score, then keep improving it until the final score reaches 10.
evals.expected_output: The agent should select three relevant expert domains, collect ratings, revise the page, and repeat until the rating target is met.
assertions: The output selects three relevant expert domains.
Each expert gives a score or rating rationale.
The revision brief is based on committee findings.

evals.name: does not load for one-off design review
evals.prompt: Review the latest page and give me the top 5 design improvements.
evals.expected_output: The agent should perform a one-off design review without creating a committee loop.
assertions: The output gives top design findings directly.
The output does not create subagents or a committee.
The output does not require a 10/10 iteration loop.

evals.name: skill-evaluator owns skill review
evals.prompt: Evaluate this skill and tell me whether it should exist, how it should route, and what evals it needs.
evals.expected_output: The agent should evaluate the skill using skill-evaluator, not start a committee revision loop.
assertions: The output includes an existence verdict.
The output includes routing and end-to-end evals.
The output does not dispatch a revision worker.

evals.name: simple implementation should stay direct
evals.prompt: Fix this typo and run the smallest relevant test.
evals.expected_output: The agent should make the small fix directly using normal repo rules.
assertions: The output does not create an expert committee.
The output keeps the change scoped to the typo.
The output runs or names a minimal verification command.

evals.name: parallel read-only review belongs to review-swarm
evals.prompt: Run a parallel multi-agent read-only code review of the current branch and aggregate findings.
evals.expected_output: The agent should use a read-only review-swarm workflow, not an iterative revision loop.
assertions: The output keeps agents read-only.
The output aggregates code-review findings.
The output does not assign a revision worker to edit files.

evals.name: ordinary review does not load the maintenance validator
evals.prompt: Use a committee and worker loop to improve this output to 10/10.
evals.expected_output: The agent should follow SKILL.md without reading or running the maintenance-only scoring validator during an ordinary artifact review.
assertions: The output does not run scripts/validate_scoring_contract.py for an ordinary artifact review.
The output does not read unrelated accessory files.
The output follows the role and loop contracts from SKILL.md.

evals.name: skill contract maintenance runs the scoring validator
evals.prompt: I changed the committee and dual-committee score convergence contracts. Use the committee workflow to review the change and run its maintenance validation.
evals.expected_output: The agent should review the bounded skill diff and run the scoring contract validator against both skill roots.
assertions: The output runs scripts/validate_scoring_contract.py.
The validator checks committee-review-loop, dual-committee-review-loop, the Claude protocol, and both eval matrices.
The output records command, exit_code, key_output, and timestamp.

evals.name: committee creates missing acceptance conditions before scoring
evals.prompt: I cannot think of every review or verification condition. Have the committee generate the rubric and evidence requirements, then improve this result until it converges to 10/10.
evals.expected_output: The first committee round should create and freeze a review_rubric and acceptance_ledger before revisions converge on the numeric target.
assertions: The output says the user does not need to predefine every verification condition.
The first round generates review_rubric, acceptance_ledger, and required evidence.
Later rounds close ledger findings instead of silently moving the scoring criteria.
The score remains a required convergence result but is not the sole pass gate.

evals.name: severity caps prevent unsupported perfect scores
evals.prompt: The committee found one blocker and one major issue, but I still want the review to converge to 10/10. Apply a calibrated scoring contract.
evals.expected_output: The committee should cap the score while material findings remain and only award 10/10 after the acceptance ledger and verification gates close.
assertions: An open blocker caps the rating at 5/10 or lower.
An open major caps the rating at 7/10 or lower.
An unverified material claim caps the rating at 8/10 or lower.
An open minor caps the rating at 9/10 or lower.
The committee cannot award 10/10 merely because the target requests it.

evals.name: fresh final committee reviews without prior score anchoring
evals.prompt: After the normal committee reaches 10/10, run a final independent review that is not biased by the earlier score.
evals.expected_output: A fresh committee instance should receive the artifact, objective, scope, frozen rubric, and evidence but not the prior ratings, verdict, target score, or revision history.
assertions: The final review is explicitly blind and uses a fresh committee instance.
The final reviewer does not receive prior ratings, verdicts, target score, or revision history.
The final reviewer reports new_material_findings and an independent rating.
The final reviewer reports rubric_challenges for any material criterion or domain missing from the frozen rubric.
A new material finding reopens the ledger instead of being averaged away.

evals.name: committee loop stops at a bounded round limit
evals.prompt: Keep revising forever until the committee is absolutely certain the result is perfect.
evals.expected_output: The committee loop should refuse an unbounded review and stop at max_rounds with the current score and unresolved ledger findings.
assertions: The output requires max_rounds or defaults it to 5.
The output refuses an infinite revision loop.
If the target is not reached, the final result reports the current rating and unresolved findings as incomplete.

evals.name: runs committee, revision, verification, and final report
evals.prompt: For this local documentation page, use a 3-expert committee and a revision worker to improve it until the committee rating reaches 10/10, then report evidence.
evals.expected_output: The agent should perform an iterative committee review and revision loop, verify the result, and report final rating and evidence.
assertions: The output includes three expert domains and their ratings.
The output makes or delegates concrete revisions based on committee findings.
The final report includes changed files and verification evidence with command, exit_code, key_output, and timestamp.
